item.title: 61 years ago this month, the Fast Fourier Transform was created, a powerful tool for image compressi...
item.description:
61 years ago this month, the Fast Fourier Transform was created, a powerful tool for image compression &amp; data analysis.
Watch a classic MIT breakdown of FFT, perhaps the most-taught algorithm at the Institute: https://t.co/R7zdspBswx
v/@MITOCW https://t.co/lVabrS3syd
--- @MIT_CSAIL | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 0

item.title: 下周也准备把 tuwa 的同步听功能单独拆分出来做成新产品，并且拓展成同步听、同步说、同步学。试试目前的工程能力能否将实时语音模型推到极致。

item.title: Continued weak spots of AI, from the point of view of a business professional and not a PhD biochemi...
item.description:
Continued weak spots of AI, from the point of view of a business professional and not a PhD biochemist:
1) SVGs. The ability to "illustrate" and have that thing be infinitely scalable. See below image. If you ask for flat PNGs, fine. ChatGPT is the best at the image game right now. Claude Design has the same SVG issue. Hard to image scalability in creative projects or game design without it.
2) Gullibility. I have had to create a specific prompt to remind Claude that what people say or type is not always true because the prompt "imagine you're a greedy capitalist and read from their perspective" isn't good enough. This is a tricky line to toe, however - I don't want Claude to be manipulative, but I need it to deeply understand the world of manipulation to better support my response (ex: email reply draft) to it.
3) Multi-layered communication. Imagine you have to turn down a client event (but it's your favorite client, and you could have technically moved your friend group trip to make it happen but you didn't want to, but just in case your client is about to fire you if you don't do it, you want to write it in a way that doesn't close that door, but you don't want to sound desperate, but you want to show your respect for them, but you also had to turn down their last thing so you have to be delicate and also your vendor is on cc so you can't say secret information but you want to hint at their release but you'd get fired if you mentioned anything, but you want to drum up excitement). Claude, which I find to be the best writer of the bunch, just says the "hidden" message out loud like an untrained intern at a cocktail party after a glass of wine. Delicate nuance is tough.
4) Humor. I know the Claude Mythos paper said it was better at humor, but the current productionized models don't seem to understand the unspoken construction of a joke. It always defaults to randomness. It assumes that non-sequitur = humor. Like if you said "I want to say something funny walked into an office" it would say "oh a flamingo with a tutu!" even though it had no relevance to the rest of the setup.
5) Extremely long context. People incorrectly believe that multi-million token conversations only happen with engineers+AI because of codebase length. As more business professionals lean into building, and as we connect more systems (and more people into single shared systems), we're going to need to find ways to manage 10M+ tokens without context degradation. Countless business use cases are held back because of this.
6) Collaboration. How is there no way to merge memory yet? How is there no no-code model fusion in market? Agent teams starts to get closer (in the sense that you can build AI personas of your board of directors and have them fight it out and review the outcome), but I want true merging and model weight changes.
--- @alliekmiller | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 107
item.author: @alliekmiller

item.title:
今天在上海参加狂喜播客节。收获蛮大。
第一次见关雅荻老师。HOLD 住全场的超级导演 + 大管家的即视感。看到关老师精神满满的状态，就知道狂喜为什么能连续办到第五届，以及很期待能持续办到至少第 10...
item.description:
今天在上海参加狂喜播客节。收获蛮大。
第一次见关雅荻老师。HOLD 住全场的超级导演 + 大管家的即视感。看到关老师精神满满的状态，就知道狂喜为什么能连续办到第五届，以及很期待能持续办到至少第 102 届。
上午在杨天楠的组织下，有幸和李楠、卫诗婕 一起聊了一期 AI 与 OPC 的话题。聊的比预期的深入好玩。李楠做 AI 硬件创业的视角、我做 AI 软件创业的视角、诗婕做媒体创业的视角，各有不同。最终却指向了相同的几个点。第一个点是要 be real，要真实。内心要真想做成某件事，要及早寻找付费客户，不怕失败的往前走，胜算会更大些。第二点是，都有点讨厌 OPC 在美元基金烘托下的要做大做强的定义。未来的世界很多元。OPC 更好的定义，不是一人公司，而是一门生意。生意是生活的意义，寻找到一个人或一个团队共同认可的生意模式，能比较好的养活自己养活团队，是更有意思的追求。
下午听了阑夕ོ 、潘乱等人的现场播客。在电影院坐看播客的感觉真爽。AI 很强，是人的放大器。台上嘉宾貌似有个共识：AI 是人的放大器，特别是对可以 SOP 的事情来说。但 AI 很难让一个人从 0 到 1 学会内容创作，比如写出阑夕一言的文字。想起卡兹克之前也分享过一个观点：在你能手搓爆款之前，不要指望通过AI 能直接出爆款。
潘乱分享了最近写出库克与苹果价值锚点那篇文章的选题过程，是在初稿立意很平庸的情况下，放弃了初稿，然后骑着外卖车沿着北京二环绕了一圈，然后才想到了价值锚点这个选题。骑行确定选题，就是“文章本天成、妙手偶得之”的那个点。
想起纵横四海的最近一期播客，也谈到类似一个观点：老是非常忙，是搞不好内容创作的。得有闲暇时间，在散步，在骑行，在发呆等情况下，妙手偶得的灵感会更多涌现并有效捕获。
阑夕说：差生文具多。古话就是经典。内容创作，会用 AI 不是关键。你脑海里的想法，你对好内容的嗅觉，你曾写出过优质内容的经验，这是 AI 依旧很难帮到你。
下午还去听了羊行参与的无用的浪漫现场播客。生活气息非常浓，好玩。然后跑到一期喜剧播客现场了，更欢乐了。可爱，好玩，乐活。
傍晚回到刘飞在场的一期播客。刘飞没怎么说话，看着比一年前年轻了很多。
春末的上海，有些微热。期待有那么一天，当你输入 boke 时，默认排第一的不是博客，而是播客。
--- @lifesinger | ❤️ 1 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 198
item.author: @lifesinger

(also the internet; there should be a protocol that is equally usable by people and agents)
